meaning: n.鞭打,打谷adj.(打击等)沉重的
examples: Then and there they gave each other a sound thrashing.
A whale was thrashing the water with its tail.
Someone was thrashing around in the water, obviously in trouble.
Gayle took a liking to Plunkett, thrashing him through the covers, down the ground and over backward point.
Israel joined GB in next year's finals with a 98-61 thrashing of the Czechs in Tel-Aviv.
